content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "field-operations",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 28,
    "domain_depth": 22,
    "data_richness": 18,
    "interaction_quality": 20,
    "ai_integration": 10,
    "mobile_first": 15,
    "performance": 30,
    "typescript_quality": 35,
    "error_handling": 18,
    "realtime": 15,
    "accessibility": 12,
    "security": 20,
    "differentiation": 10,
    "enterprise_readiness": 15
  },
  "overall_score": 19,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C6-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Daily Log page missing legally defensible structure with weather auto-population, crew tables, and 90-second submission flow",
      "file": "src/pages/DailyLog.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires: weather auto-populated from API, manpower table by trade/company/headcount/hours, 'Same as yesterday' button, voice-to-text, 'No incidents today' default toggle, draft/submitted/approved status workflow, calendar view showing log gaps. Current page lacks most of these construction-specific daily log sections and speed optimizations. A superintendent cannot complete a log in under 90 seconds.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, completely rebuild the DailyLog component to match PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 7. BEFORE: The page has a basic form or placeholder that does not include auto-populated weather from the weather_cache table, a manpower-by-trade table with running totals, 'Same as yesterday' clone button, default-ON toggles for 'No incidents today' and 'No visitors today', voice-to-text icons on text inputs, photo multi-select upload, or draft/submitted/approved status workflow. It does not query the daily_logs table from Supabase or show a calendar view of which days have logs. AFTER: The page should (1) query Supabase daily_logs table for the current project with real-time subscription, (2) auto-populate weather from weather_cache or weather_records table on page load with an 'Auto' badge, (3) show a manpower section as an editable table with columns: Trade, Company, Headcount, Hours, with a running total row, (4) include a 'Same as yesterday' button that clones crew/equipment/visitors from the prior day's log, (5) default 'No incidents today' and 'No visitors today' toggles to ON so those sections are collapsed by default, (6) show a sticky 'Submit Log' button at the bottom, (7) include a calendar view toggle showing green/yellow/orange/red dots for approved/submitted/draft/missing days, (8) use skeleton loaders while data loads, (9) show a contextual empty state 'No daily logs yet. The daily log is your project's official record.' with 'Start Today's Log' CTA, (10) format all dates without hyphens. Use theme tokens from src/styles/theme.ts for all styling. Query supabase from src/lib/supabase.ts. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C6-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Safety page missing OSHA-compliant incident tracking, TRIR calculation, inspection templates, and certification expiration alerts",
      "file": "src/pages/Safety.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 11 requires: Days Since Last Incident counter, TRIR auto-calculation from incident and manpower data, incident creation with severity levels matching OSHA (first_aid/medical_treatment/lost_time/fatality), corrective action tracking with assignee and due date, toolbox talk attendance with digital sign-in, certification expiration alerts at 90/60/30 days, and inspection templates. Current page lacks these domain-specific workflows.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, rebuild the Safety component to implement PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 11. BEFORE: The page shows basic placeholder content or a simple list without OSHA-specific incident severity classification, TRIR calculation, corrective action workflow, toolbox talk attendance tracking, certification expiration monitoring, or inspection scheduling. It does not query the incidents, safety_inspections, safety_certifications, toolbox_talks, toolbox_talk_attendees, or corrective_actions tables from Supabase. AFTER: The page should (1) show 5 metric cards at top: 'Days Since Last Incident' (resets on medical_treatment or higher severity), TRIR calculated as (recordable incidents x 200000 / total hours worked), 'Open Corrective Actions' count, 'Inspections This Week' count, 'Certifications Expiring Soon' count, (2) query the incidents table with color-coded severity: first_aid=yellow, medical_treatment=orange, lost_time=red, fatality=black, (3) show a corrective_actions list with assignee, due date, status, and overdue highlighting, (4) include a toolbox_talks section showing recent talks with attendee count, (5) show safety_certifications with expiration badges: green=>90 days, amber=30-90 days, red=<30 days, black=expired, (6) use skeleton loaders, (7) show contextual empty state per section, (8) use real-time subscriptions on incidents and corrective_actions tables. Use theme tokens from src/styles/theme.ts. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C6-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Field Capture page missing camera-first workflow, GPS auto-tagging, drawing pin linking, and offline queue",
      "file": "src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 14 requires: camera opens immediately on 'Capture' tap, auto-metadata (GPS, timestamp, weather), post-capture form with optional title/notes/tags/link-to-entity, drawing pin placement when linked to a drawing, AI photo analysis suggestions, offline capture queue with sync indicator, and time-lapse view for same-location photos. Current implementation lacks the camera-first UX and entity linking.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, rebuild the FieldCapture component to match PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA section 14. BEFORE: The page shows a basic photo gallery or placeholder without camera-first capture workflow, GPS auto-tagging via navigator.geolocation, post-capture metadata form, entity linking (to drawings/tasks/punch items/RFIs/daily logs), or offline queue visibility. It does not query field_captures or photo_pins tables from Supabase. AFTER: The page should (1) show a prominent 'Capture' button that triggers file input with accept='image/*' and capture='environment' for mobile camera access, (2) after photo selection show a post-capture overlay form with: optional Title input, optional Notes textarea with microphone icon for voice-to-text placeholder, Tags multi-select, 'Link to' searchable dropdown (drawing/task/punch/RFI/daily log), and 'Save' button, (3) auto-tag each capture with timestamp and GPS coordinates from navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition, (4) display a chronological photo grid feed with metadata overlays (date, time, location tag), (5) show a map view toggle using project lat/lon with photo pins plotted by GPS, (6) include an offline indicator banner 'You are offline, X photos pending upload' when navigator.onLine is false, (7) show contextual empty state 'No field captures yet. Start documenting site conditions with photos.' with 'Open Camera' CTA, (8) query Supabase field_captures or files table filtered to field photos for the current project with real-time subscription. Use theme tokens. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C6-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "ai_integration",
      "title": "No AI daily summary generation or AI photo analysis wired to edge functions",
      "file": "src/pages/DailyLog.tsx",
      "description": "PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P0-4 and MODULE_SPECS require AI auto-summary of daily log narratives and AI photo analysis for safety violations. No edge function calls exist for ai-daily-summary or AI-powered field photo analysis. The Daily Log should have a 'Generate AI Summary' button that calls the ai-daily-summary edge function and populates the work summary field.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, add an AI summary generation feature to the daily log form. BEFORE: There is no AI integration in the daily log. No button calls the ai-daily-summary edge function. The work summary field must be typed manually. AFTER: Add a 'Generate Summary' button (with a sparkle/wand icon from lucide-react) next to or above the work summary textarea. When clicked, it should (1) set a loading state with the button showing a spinner, (2) call supabase.functions.invoke('ai-daily-summary', { body: { projectId, logDate, field_notes: currentWorkSummary, temperature_high, temperature_low, crew_count } }), (3) on success, populate the work summary textarea with the returned summary text and show a toast 'AI summary generated', (4) on error, show a toast 'Could not generate summary. Try again.' and re-enable the button, (5) style the button as a secondary/ghost button with the sparkle icon using theme.colors.primaryOrange for the icon color. The button should be disabled when the daily log is in 'approved' status. If the edge function does not exist yet, handle the 404 gracefully with a toast 'AI summary is not available yet'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "field-operations-C6-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Daily Log and Field Capture lack mobile-optimized touch targets, sticky submit buttons, and voice-to-text affordances",
      "file": "src/pages/DailyLog.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires 44x44px minimum touch targets, sticky submit button always visible, voice-to-text microphone icons on every text input, camera integration for quick photo capture, and single-column layout on mobile. Current forms do not have responsive breakpoints, sticky positioning on submit, or microphone button affordances for field use with gloved hands.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, add mobile-first field optimizations to the daily log component. BEFORE: The form inputs do not have minimum 44px touch target height, the submit button is not sticky at the bottom of the viewport, there are no microphone icons next to text inputs for voice-to-text, and the layout does not adapt to single-column on screens under 768px. AFTER: (1) Set all input and button elements to minHeight: 44 using theme spacing, (2) wrap the 'Submit Log' button in a div with position: 'sticky', bottom: 0, zIndex: 10, padding: theme.spacing.md, background: theme.colors.surface, borderTop: `1px solid ${theme.colors.border}`, boxShadow: theme.shadows.md so it is always visible while scrolling, (3) add a microphone button (Mic icon from lucide-react, 44x44px touch target) next to the work summary textarea and the issues/delays textarea. On click, show a toast 'Voice input coming soon' as a placeholder. Style the mic button with background: theme.colors.backgroundAlt, borderRadius: borderRadius.md, border: `1px solid ${theme.colors.border}`, (4) add a CSS media query via inline style conditional (check window.innerWidth < 768 or use a useMediaQuery hook) to stack all form sections in a single column on mobile, (5) ensure all form labels are above inputs (never beside) and use fontSize: theme.typography.fontSize.sm with color: theme.colors.textSecondary. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Smart Daily Log with Predictive Crew Scheduling",
      "description": "AI analyzes past 30 days of daily logs to predict tomorrow's crew needs based on schedule phase, weather forecast, and historical patterns. Shows 'Recommended crew for tomorrow' card at top of daily log with one-tap confirmation. Reduces superintendent planning time from 15 minutes to 30 seconds.",
      "effort": "medium",
      "impact": "high"
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's daily log is functional but requires many taps and has no AI summary or weather auto-population. Fieldwire excels at photo capture with offline sync but has no daily log or safety module at all. Buildertrend has basic daily logs but no OSHA compliance features. SiteSync's opportunity is to combine Fieldwire's camera-first speed with Procore's documentation depth and add AI intelligence that neither has. The 90-second daily log target would be a genuine competitive advantage since Procore's daily log typically takes 5-10 minutes.",
  "summary": "Field Operations scores critically low across all dimensions because the three core pages (Daily Log, Safety, Field Capture) lack the construction-domain-specific workflows that PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA mandates. The Daily Log is missing legally defensible structure including weather auto-population, manpower-by-trade tables, draft/submit/approve workflow, and the 90-second submission speed target. Safety has no OSHA-compliant incident severity classification, TRIR calculation, corrective action tracking, or certification expiration alerts. Field Capture lacks the camera-first UX, GPS auto-tagging, and drawing pin linking that make it useful in the field. None of the three pages query real Supabase data despite existing tables (daily_logs, incidents, safety_inspections, corrective_actions, toolbox_talks, field_captures). The five prompts focus on rebuilding these pages with full domain depth, wiring to Supabase, adding AI summary generation via edge functions, and implementing mobile-first optimizations (sticky submit, 44px touch targets, voice-to-text affordances) that a superintendent needs in the field."
}
```
